summary: Implement Express error-handling middleware and consistent error responses
problem_statement: The server lacks a centralised error-handling strategy. Route handlers catch and respond to errors independently with inconsistent JSON shapes, status codes, and levels of detail. Some errors go unhandled entirely and crash the process. A dedicated Express error-handling middleware provides a single place to catch, format, and respond to errors consistently.
in_scope: Implement a typed Express error-handling middleware (err, req, res, next) as the final middleware in the stack
Define a consistent JSON error response shape for all handled 4xx and 5xx errors
Define a custom/typed error class (e.g. AppError) that route handlers can throw with status code and message
Wire the middleware into the Express app after all routes
Ensure unhandled errors return a generic 500 response without leaking stack traces in production
out_of_scope: Client-side error handling — React ErrorBoundary is handled in P6-R2-18
Fixing the rethrow-after-response bug — handled separately in P6-R2-20
Request logging changes — handled in P6-R2-21
requirements: An Express error-handling middleware is registered as the final middleware
All error responses follow a consistent JSON shape (e.g. { error: string, status: number, details?: unknown })
A custom error class exists for throwing typed errors with HTTP status codes from route handlers
Stack traces are not leaked in production responses
acceptance_criteria: Server error responses follow a consistent JSON shape for handled 4xx/5xx responses
A custom error class is available for route handlers to throw structured errors
Unhandled errors return a generic 500 without leaking internals
npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
dependencies: P2-R2-07 — server route typing should be in place
risks: Existing route handlers may send responses before throwing, which the middleware cannot intercept — coordinate with P6-R2-20
implementation_notes: Express error-handling middleware must have exactly four parameters (err, req, res, next). Place it after all route registrations. The custom error class can extend Error with a statusCode property. Use the NODE_ENV environment variable to gate stack trace inclusion in responses.
